meaning: adj.学校的,学校教育的,学者的,学究的,经院的或经院哲学家的n.经院哲学家,学究,墨守成规者,学生
examples: Some of these gifted people may have done poorly in school because their, gifts were not scholastic.
The results of the experiments where scholastic performance was rewarded were uniformly disappointing.
Parkin's characterization of the movement as Neo-Scholastic is too cursory to be accepted without further investigation.
Between them, Bloomsbury and Scholastic printed a first edition of more than four million copies.
Children in Asia and Europe often trounce their American counterparts in standardised scholastic tests.
